1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
8		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
9	)
10	
11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
91		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
92		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
93			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
94		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
95		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98	
99		// Register "ddx work plan" and "ddx work focus" as subcommands of "ddx work".
100		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
101		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
102	
103		return cmd
104	}
105	
106	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
107	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
108	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
109	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
110	// by this layer.
111	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
113			return err
114		}
115	
116		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
120		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
121		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123		if !asJSON {
124			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
125			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
126				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
127			})
128		}
129	
130		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
131	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7		"text/tabwriter"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
12		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
13	)
14	
15	// newWorkPlanCommand creates the "ddx work plan" subcommand: a read-only
16	// dry-run preview of the queue showing the exact ordering "ddx work" would
17	// pick, without claiming or executing any bead.
18	//
19	// Relationship to siblings:
20	//   - "ddx work"       — executes the drain (claims and runs beads)
21	//   - "ddx work plan"  — previews what the drain would do (read-only)
22	//   - "ddx bead ready" — lists dependency-ready beads; does NOT apply the
23	//     picker's label-filter or cooldown/superseded eligibility filters
24	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkPlanCommand() *cobra.Command {
25		cmd := &cobra.Command{
26			Use:   "plan",
27			Short: "Preview what 'ddx work' would pick (dry-run)",
28			Long: `plan shows the execution queue in picker order — the same order
29	'ddx work' would claim beads — without claiming or executing anything.
30	
31	Unlike 'ddx bead ready', plan applies the picker's filters:
32	  - Execution eligibility (cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible flag)
33	  - Optional label-filter intersection (--label-filter)
34	  - Optional capabilities constraint (--capabilities; currently a no-op)
35	
36	The per-Run attempted map is intentionally excluded: it is non-deterministic
37	across runs and is not part of the stable picker decision surface.
38	
39	Columns:
40	  POS      — 1-based pick order
41	  ID       — bead ID
42	  TITLE    — bead title
43	  PRI      — priority (0 = highest)
44	  RANK     — queue-rank within the priority bucket, when present
45	  UPDATED  — last updated timestamp (RFC3339)
46	  STATUS   — bead status
47	  DECISION — "next claim", "eligible (rank N)", or "skipped: <reason>"
48	
49	Use --json for machine-readable output suitable for piping to jq:
50	  ddx work plan --json | jq '.[].id'
51	`,
52			Example: `  # Show top 10 beads in picker order (default)
53	  ddx work plan
54	
55	  # Show the full queue
56	  ddx work plan --limit=0
57	
58	  # Filter identically to a label-constrained worker
59	  ddx work plan --label-filter=phase:2,area:agent
60	
61	  # Machine-readable output
62	  ddx work plan --json | jq '.[].id'`,
63			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
64			RunE: f.runWorkPlan,
65		}
66	
67		cmd.Flags().String("label-filter", "", "Filter by label (same intersection logic as 'ddx work --label-filter')")
68		cmd.Flags().String("capabilities", "", "Capabilities constraint (reserved; currently no-op pass-through)")
69		cmd.Flags().Int("limit", 10, "Maximum number of entries to show; 0 = full queue")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output as JSON array (suitable for piping to jq)")
71	
72		return cmd
73	}
74	
75	// runWorkPlan is the RunE for "ddx work plan".
76	func (f *CommandFactory) runWorkPlan(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
77		labelFilter, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("label-filter")
78		capabilities, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("capabilities")
79		limit, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("limit")
80		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
81	
82		// Resolve the project root (same logic work.go uses).
83		projectRoot := f.WorkingDir
84	
85		// Open the bead store directly — same path ReadyExecution uses.
86		ddxDir := projectRoot + "/.ddx"
87		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
88	
89		filters := agent.PickerFilters{
90			LabelFilter:  labelFilter,
91			Capabilities: capabilities,
92		}
93	
94		entries, err := agent.PreviewQueue(store, filters, limit)
95		if err != nil {
96			return fmt.Errorf("work plan: %w", err)
97		}
98		var breakdown *bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown
99		if diag, ok := any(store).(interface {
100			ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
101		}); ok {
102			if b, derr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); derr == nil {
103				breakdown = &b
104			}
105		}
106	
107		if asJSON {
108			return printWorkPlanJSON(cmd, entries)
109		}
110		return printWorkPlanText(cmd, entries, breakdown, limit)
111	}
112	
113	func printWorkPlanJSON(cmd *cobra.Command, entries []agent.QueueEntry) error {
114		enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
115		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
116		return enc.Encode(entries)
117	}
118	
119	func printWorkPlanText(cmd *cobra.Command, entries []agent.QueueEntry, breakdown *bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, limit int) error {
120		out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
121		if len(entries) == 0 {
122			fmt.Fprintln(out, "No execution-eligible beads in the queue.")
123		}
124	
125		w := tabwriter.NewWriter(out, 0, 0, 2, ' ', 0)
126		fmt.Fprintln(w, "POS\tID\tTITLE\tPRI\tRANK\tUPDATED\tSTATUS\tDECISION\tWHY")
127		fmt.Fprintln(w, "---\t--\t-----\t---\t----\t-------\t------\t--------\t---")
128		for _, e := range entries {
129			updated := e.UpdatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
130			if e.UpdatedAt.IsZero() {
131				updated = "-"
132			}
133			rank := "-"
134			if e.QueueRank != nil {
135				rank = fmt.Sprintf("%d", *e.QueueRank)
136			}
137			fmt.Fprintf(w, "%d\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n",
138				e.Position, e.BeadID, e.Title, e.Priority, rank, updated, e.Status, e.FilterDecision, e.Why)
139		}
140		_ = w.Flush()
141	
142		if breakdown != nil {
143			if len(breakdown.Epics) > 0 {
144				fmt.Fprintf(out, "  skipped %d ready epic(s) with open children (epics are structural containers; decompose into tasks): %s\n",
145					len(breakdown.Epics), strings.Join(breakdown.Epics, ", "))
146			}
147			if len(breakdown.EpicClosureCandidates) > 0 {
148				fmt.Fprintf(out, "  completed epic closure candidate(s) (all direct children closed; surfaced for closure evaluation): %s\n",
149					strings.Join(breakdown.EpicClosureCandidates, ", "))
150			}
151		}
152	
153		if limit > 0 {
154			fmt.Fprintf(out, "\n(showing up to %d entries; use --limit=0 for full queue)\n", limit)
155		}
156		return nil
157	}

9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.

106		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
107		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
108	)

110	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
111	const (
112		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
113		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
114		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
115		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
116		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
117		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
118		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
119	)

121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}
145	
146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}

186		DefaultStatus   = StatusOpen
187		DefaultPriority = 2
188		DefaultPrefix   = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
189		MinPriority     = 0
190		MaxPriority     = 4
191	)

241	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
242	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
243		var ids []string
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
246		}
247		return ids
248	}
249	
250	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
251	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			if d.DependsOnID == id {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		return false
258	}
259	
260	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
261	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
262		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
263			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
264				return // already exists
265			}
266		}
267		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
268			IssueID:     b.ID,
269			DependsOnID: depID,
270			Type:        depType,
271			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
272		})
273	}
274	
275	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
276	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
277		var filtered []Dependency
278		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
279			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
280				filtered = append(filtered, d)
281			}
282		}
283		b.Dependencies = filtered
284	}

5	// ParkReason identifies the canonical reason for transitioning a bead to
6	// proposed status for operator attention. Each constant maps to a fixed
7	// (Reason, Source) pair used by ParkToProposed.
8	type ParkReason string
9	
10	const (
11		ParkIntakeRejection           ParkReason = "intake_rejection"
12		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired ParkReason = "no_changes_operator_required"
13		ParkPostReviewMalfunction     ParkReason = "post_review_malfunction"
14		ParkReviewTerminal            ParkReason = "review_terminal"
15		ParkConflictRecovery          ParkReason = "conflict_recovery"
16	)
17	
18	type parkReasonMeta struct {
19		Reason string
20		Source string
21	}
22	
23	var parkReasonMetaMap = map[ParkReason]parkReasonMeta{
24		ParkIntakeRejection:           {Reason: "pre-claim intake blocked execution", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
25		ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired: {Reason: "operator decision required before another automated attempt", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
26		ParkPostReviewMalfunction:     {Reason: "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
27		ParkReviewTerminal:            {Reason: "terminal review block requires operator decision", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
28		ParkConflictRecovery:          {Reason: "land conflict requires operator judgment", Source: "ddx agent execute-loop"},
29	}
30	
31	// ParkToProposed transitions the bead to proposed status for operator
32	// attention. It enforces OperatorRequired=true and the canonical Reason and
33	// Source from the ParkReason mapping. The mutate callback runs after the
34	// status transition; pass nil if no additional metadata is needed.
35	func (s *Store) ParkToProposed(id string, reason ParkReason, mutate func(*Bead)) error {
36		meta, ok := parkReasonMetaMap[reason]
37		if !ok {
38			return fmt.Errorf("bead: unknown ParkReason %q", reason)
39		}
40		return s.TransitionLifecycle(id, StatusProposed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
41			OperatorRequired: true,
42			Reason:           meta.Reason,
43			Source:           meta.Source,
44		}, func(b *Bead) error {
45			if mutate != nil {
46				mutate(b)
47			}
48			return nil
49		})
50	}

```go
func activeRetryCooldown(b Bead, now time.Time) (time.Time, bool) {
    retryAfterStr := extraStringVal(b.Extra, ExtraRetryAfter)
    if retryAfterStr == "" {
        return time.Time{}, false
    }
    retryAfter, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, retryAfterStr)
    if err != nil {
        return time.Time{}, false
    }
    return retryAfter, retryAfter.After(now)
}
```

106	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
107	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
108	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
109	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
110	// by this layer.
111	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
113			return err
114		}
115	
116		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
120		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
121		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123		if !asJSON {
124			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
125			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
126				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
127			})
128		}
129	
130		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
131	}

20	// TestMain clears all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
21	// that tests invoking git commands operate on their own temp-dir repositories
22	// rather than any repository inherited from a hook invocation (e.g. GIT_DIR
23	// set by lefthook during pre-commit). Prefix scrub is important — lefthook
24	// sets GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL, GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS etc. that
25	// also leak into test subprocesses and corrupt the shared repo config.
26	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
27		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
30					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
31				}
32			}
33		}
34		cleanupTemp := isolateCmdTestTempRoot()
35		code := m.Run()
36		cleanupTemp()
37		os.Exit(code)

52			} else {

54			}

package cmd

import (
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func setupCooldownEnv(t *testing.T, beads ...*bead.Bead) (*TestEnvironment, *bead.Store) {
	t.Helper()
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(env.Dir, ".ddx"))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	for _, b := range beads {
		require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
	}
	return env, store
}

func TestClearCooldowns_All(t *testing.T) {
	b1 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-cc-1", Title: "Bead one"}
	b2 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-cc-2", Title: "Bead two"}
	b3 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-cc-3", Title: "Bead three (no cooldown)"}

	env, store := setupCooldownEnv(t, b1, b2, b3)

	until := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(b1.ID, until, "push_failed", "push rejected"))
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(b2.ID, until, "no_changes_unjustified", "no changes"))

	root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "clear-cooldowns", "--all")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleared 2 cooldown(s)")

	got1, err := store.Get(b1.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, got1.Extra[bead.ExtraRetryAfter], "retry-after should be cleared on b1")
	assert.Empty(t, got1.Extra[bead.ExtraLastStatus], "last-status should be cleared on b1")
	assert.Empty(t, got1.Extra[bead.ExtraLastDetail], "last-detail should be cleared on b1")

	got2, err := store.Get(b2.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, got2.Extra[bead.ExtraRetryAfter], "retry-after should be cleared on b2")

	got3, err := store.Get(b3.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, got3.Extra[bead.ExtraRetryAfter], "b3 had no cooldown; still empty")
}

func TestClearCooldowns_ByStatus(t *testing.T) {
	b1 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-cs-1", Title: "Push failed bead"}
	b2 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-cs-2", Title: "No changes bead"}
	b3 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-cs-3", Title: "Another push failed bead"}

	env, store := setupCooldownEnv(t, b1, b2, b3)

	until := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(b1.ID, until, "push_failed", "detail a"))
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(b2.ID, until, "no_changes_unjustified", "detail b"))
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(b3.ID, until, "push_failed", "detail c"))

	root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "clear-cooldowns", "--status", "push_failed")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleared 2 cooldown(s)")

	got1, err := store.Get(b1.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, got1.Extra[bead.ExtraRetryAfter], "b1 push_failed cooldown should be cleared")

	got2, err := store.Get(b2.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, got2.Extra[bead.ExtraRetryAfter], "b2 no_changes cooldown should remain")

	got3, err := store.Get(b3.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, got3.Extra[bead.ExtraRetryAfter], "b3 push_failed cooldown should be cleared")
}

func TestClearCooldowns_DryRun(t *testing.T) {
	b1 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-dr-1", Title: "Dry run bead one"}
	b2 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-dr-2", Title: "Dry run bead two"}

	env, store := setupCooldownEnv(t, b1, b2)

	until := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(b1.ID, until, "push_failed", "detail"))
	require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(b2.ID, until, "push_failed", "detail"))

	root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "clear-cooldowns", "--all", "--dry-run")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "would clear 2 cooldown(s)")

	// Verify no mutation occurred
	got1, err := store.Get(b1.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, got1.Extra[bead.ExtraRetryAfter], "b1 cooldown must not be cleared in dry-run")

	got2, err := store.Get(b2.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, got2.Extra[bead.ExtraRetryAfter], "b2 cooldown must not be cleared in dry-run")

	// Verify the output says "would clear" not "cleared"
	assert.False(t, strings.Contains(out, "cleared "), "dry-run must not say 'cleared'")
}
